<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [warning] All 25 test-to-source imports target llm-provider-core exclusively, confirming the utility layer (src-2) currently has no dedicated unit tests.
[observation] [info] Perfect cohesion and zero coupling are the gold standard for a test zone — this suite is cleanly isolated from all non-test concerns.
[observation] [info] codex-cli-provider.ts and llm-config.ts do not appear to have corresponding test files; verify coverage parity across all provider implementations.
[relationship] [warning] Test coverage is structurally biased toward llm-provider-core; no test files import from llm-runtime-support, creating a coverage boundary that coincides with the zone boundary — adding a tests/unit/support/ directory would close this gap and make the zone boundary explicit in the test layout.
[anti-pattern] [warning] codex-cli-provider.ts is the only concrete provider implementation without a dedicated test file; api-provider.test.ts and cli-provider.test.ts exist but codex-cli-provider.test.ts does not. Since codex-cli-provider.ts uses raw spawn and inline config resolution (diverging from the shared utilities used by other providers), it has the highest behavioral complexity and the lowest test safety net.
[suggestion] [warning] No test exercises the defaultRegistry codex factory path or verifies that createDefaultRegistry() returns a valid LLMProvider for the codex vendor — add a test to provider-registry.test.ts that calls createDefaultRegistry().create('codex', {...}) and asserts info.vendor === 'codex' to create a regression guard around the documented temporary wrapper.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling confirm the test zone is fully self-contained and correctly structured as a leaf in the dependency graph.
- 25 imports from tests into src-core with zero reverse imports is the ideal test-to-source relationship — no production code depends on tests.
- One-to-one file correspondence between tests and source modules (9 test files for 12+ source files) suggests slight gaps; provider-session and token-usage have tests, but codex-cli-provider and llm-config may not.
- Perfect cohesion and zero coupling are the gold standard for a test zone — this suite is cleanly isolated from all non-test concerns.
- codex-cli-provider.ts and llm-config.ts do not appear to have corresponding test files; verify coverage parity across all provider implementations.
- All 25 test-to-source imports target llm-provider-core exclusively, confirming the utility layer (src-2) currently has no dedicated unit tests.
- The test zone's exclusive targeting of llm-provider-core (25 imports, 0 to support) creates an implicit architectural split: core is 'tested and stable', support is 'untested and volatile' — this asymmetry increases risk for the support zone during refactoring.
- Test coverage is structurally biased toward llm-provider-core; no test files import from llm-runtime-support, creating a coverage boundary that coincides with the zone boundary — adding a tests/unit/support/ directory would close this gap and make the zone boundary explicit in the test layout.
- codex-cli-provider.ts has no corresponding test file in the test zone despite being one of the two concrete provider implementations — the gap is asymmetric since api-provider.ts and cli-provider.ts both have tests, leaving the codex provider as the only untested provider.
- codex-cli-provider.ts is the only concrete provider implementation without a dedicated test file; api-provider.test.ts and cli-provider.test.ts exist but codex-cli-provider.test.ts does not. Since codex-cli-provider.ts uses raw spawn and inline config resolution (diverging from the shared utilities used by other providers), it has the highest behavioral complexity and the lowest test safety net.
- The codex provider registered in createDefaultRegistry() explicitly declares capabilities: [] and omits validateAuth(), while the comment acknowledges it as a temporary wrapper pending a real LLMProvider implementation — but no corresponding test exercises the codex registry path. The absence of any test for the defaultRegistry codex branch means the documented tech-debt shim has no regression guard.
- No test exercises the defaultRegistry codex factory path or verifies that createDefaultRegistry() returns a valid LLMProvider for the codex vendor — add a test to provider-registry.test.ts that calls createDefaultRegistry().create('codex', {...}) and asserts info.vendor === 'codex' to create a regression guard around the documented temporary wrapper.
- [call graph] 78 internal calls, 88 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 0.47, coupling: 0.53)

</insights>
